Politics.headline: Trump Endorses Republican Plan to End DHS Shutdown
Politics.detail: After weeks of negotiations with Democrats, Republicans say they'll pass a party-line bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol, which would end the partial shutdown.

Politics.headline: US Supreme Court rejects Colorado ban on LGBTQ child 'conversion therapy'
Politics.detail: In an eight-to-one decision, the high court ruled against a law banning the discredited practice on free speech grounds.

Politics.headline: Trump attends Supreme Court hearing in unusual role: Silent observer
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump has attended oral arguments at the U.S. Supreme Court, marking a first for a sitting president.

Politics.headline: UK to host meeting of 35 countries on reopening Strait of Hormuz
Politics.detail: Before talks with 35 countries, UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer says reopening strait 'will not be easy'.

Politics.headline: Supreme Court casts doubt on Trump's order to end birthright citizenship
Politics.detail: The Supreme Court on Wednesday appeared open to invalidating President Trump's executive order that would end birthright citizenship.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil tops $116 a barrel as Iran accuses US of preparing invasion
Finance & Economy.detail: Crude prices continue to climb as world faces its biggest energy crisis in decades.

Finance & Economy.headline: Australia to halve fuel tax as global energy crisis deepens
Finance & Economy.detail: The Australian government is taking measures to alleviate the impact of rising fuel costs on its citizens amidst a global energy crisis.

Finance & Economy.headline: Philippine transport strikers say Marcos Jr failing to control oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: Transport workers in the Philippines are protesting what they perceive as the government's failure to manage escalating oil prices.

Finance & Economy.headline: Wall Street marks its worst week since start of conflict in Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: Wall Street experienced its most significant weekly decline since the commencement of the conflict in Iran, reflecting investor concerns and market volatility.

Finance & Economy.headline: Retail sales were up in February, but Middle East war may drag them down
Finance & Economy.detail: Retail sales saw an increase in February, but the ongoing war in the Middle East could potentially dampen consumer spending in the near future.

Technology.headline: Cognichip raises $60M to reinvent chip design with physics-inspired AI models
Technology.detail: Cognichip has secured $60 million in funding to advance physics-informed chip design using AI, aiming to significantly reduce chip design timelines. The investment round was led by Seligman Ventures, with participation from Mayfield, Lux Capital, and Intel's CEO Lip-Bu Tan.

Technology.headline: SpaceX reportedly files for $75B public offering that could value it at $1.75T
Technology.detail: SpaceX has reportedly filed paperwork for an IPO that could value the company at $1.75 trillion. The company is aiming to raise $75 billion through the offering.

Technology.headline: Intel to repurchase Apollo's stake in Ireland fab for $14.2B
Technology.detail: Intel is set to buy back Apollo's stake in their Ireland-based fab for $14.2 billion. This move signifies Intel's continued investment in its manufacturing capabilities.

Technology.headline: Scientists Just Broke the Solar Power Limit Everyone Thought Was Absolute
Technology.detail: Researchers have achieved a breakthrough in solar power technology that could potentially exceed 100% efficiency through an 'energy-multiplying' approach. This new method could revolutionize how we capture and utilize solar energy.

Technology.headline: Micron Revenue Smashes Expectations, but Wall Street Yawns
Technology.detail: Micron Technology's recent quarter saw them exceeding revenue and earnings expectations, however, the market's response has been muted. The Motley Fool analysts discussed whether this indicates a shift in how memory companies are perceived.

Science.headline: Artemis II sends humans around the moon for first time since Apollo
Science.detail: Artemis II is underway, carrying four astronauts around the moon on a 10-day mission to test systems for later lunar flights and an eventual moon base.

Science.headline: NASA launches humans to moon for first time in half-century
Science.detail: NASA's Space Launch System rocket lifted off, marking the start of the 10-day Artemis II mission, sending astronauts to the moon for the first time in over 50 years.

Science.headline: Scientists Found a Baby Dinosaur Hidden in Rock and It Is Surprisingly Cute
Science.detail: Scientists uncovered a rare baby dinosaur in South Korea and named it Doolysaurus after a famous cartoon character.

Science.headline: Just 10,000 quantum bits might crack internet encryption schemes
Science.detail: A recent article discusses how just 10,000 quantum bits might be enough to crack internet encryption schemes.

Science.headline: Artemis II astronauts reach orbit on historic mission to moon and back
Science.detail: Four astronauts launched aboard NASA's Artemis II mission on Thursday from Kennedy Space Centre in Florida, marking humanity's first journey to the Moon's vicinity since Apollo 17 in 1972.

Health.headline: FDA Okays Orforglipron for Weight Management
Health.detail: Eli Lilly's orforglipron has been approved for adults with obesity or overweight with at least one comorbidity, offering a new option for weight management.

Health.headline: CDC pauses dozens of types of lab testing during evaluation and in wake of downsizing
Health.detail: The CDC has temporarily stopped its diagnostic testing for various infectious diseases, including rabies and monkeypox, as it undergoes evaluation and downsizing.

Health.headline: Sleeping 11 minutes longer each night shows surprising health benefit, study finds
Health.detail: A recent study indicates that extending sleep by just 11 minutes each night can lead to notable improvements in overall health and well-being.

Health.headline: Health Clinics Fret Title X Grants Won't Be Renewed By Today's Deadline
Health.detail: Federally funded health clinics are concerned about the potential non-renewal of Title X grants, which could impact their ability to provide low-cost care.

Health.headline: AI-powered stroke tool linked to improved patient outcomes in large…
Health.detail: A new study suggests that the use of AI-powered tools in stroke diagnosis and treatment is associated with improved outcomes for patients.

Sports.headline: Celtics score 53 points in 1st quarter at Miami, setting club record for opening period
Sports.detail: The Boston Celtics exploded for 53 points in the first quarter against the Miami Heat, establishing a new franchise record for the most points scored in an opening period. This performance highlighted their offensive firepower and set the tone for the game.

Sports.headline: Sameer Rizvi Shines As Delhi Capitals Beat LSG By 6 Wickets
Sports.detail: Sameer Rizvi's impressive performance helped the Delhi Capitals secure a 6-wicket victory over the Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026. His contribution was crucial in the team's win.

Sports.headline: Woods turns down Ryder Cup captaincy as he is granted permission to leave US
Sports.detail: Tiger Woods declined the Ryder Cup captaincy and has been granted permission to leave the US. The reasons for these decisions were not specified in the summaries.

Sports.headline: Essendon coach Brad Scott has admitted he’ll be tempted to recall several key Bombers on the road back from injury
Sports.detail: Essendon coach Brad Scott is considering recalling key players returning from injury as the team evaluates reinforcements for Nate Caddy, who suffered a concussion. Sullivan Robey impressed in his VFL debut, and Jordan Ridley also played minutes, but Scott emphasizes a long-term perspective in the decision-making process.

Sports.headline: '12 months to live': Wayne Bennett's touching act as Souths great Nathan Merritt opens up battle that has 'taken my soul'
Sports.detail: Rabbitohs legend Nathan Merritt, battling stage four cancer, will ring the Legacy Bell at the game against the Bulldogs, with the try-scoring machine revealing he has 12 months to live. Wayne Bennett showed a touching act of support for Merritt.

Entertainment.headline: Megan Thee Stallion takes 2 Broadway shows off after illness during 'Moulin Rouge!'
Entertainment.detail: Megan Thee Stallion was rushed to the hospital after feeling very ill onstage during “Moulin Rouge! The Musical” on Broadway and had to take shows off.

Entertainment.headline: JAY-Z Breaks Silence on Being Accused in Diddy Lawsuits
Entertainment.detail: JAY-Z breaks his silence about being named in a 2024 sexual misconduct lawsuit tied to Sean 'Diddy' Combes in a new 'GQ' interview.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris Family Rips A.I. Posts About His Death
Entertainment.detail: Chuck Norris's family is speaking out against false reports generated by Artificial Intelligence about his death.

Entertainment.headline: अमिताभ Bachchan told me I wasn't being nice to him in Baghban: Divya Dutta says, 'I couldn't distinguish reel from real'
Entertainment.detail: Divya Dutta recalls her experience on the sets of Baghban, and how she struggled to separate her on-screen character from real life while working with Amitabh Bachchan.

Entertainment.headline: PixVerse Updates R1 Real-time World Model with Shared Worlds and Personalized Avatars
Entertainment.detail: PixVerse announced a major evolution of R1, its real-time world model, where users can now create a digital representation of themselves, place it inside the newly generated world, and share that experience with others in real time.

Crime & Justice.headline: Grizzlies' Brandon Clarke arrested in Arkansas on drug and speeding charges
Crime & Justice.detail: Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke was arrested Wednesday in Arkansas on charges including speeding and possession of a controlled substance.

Crime & Justice.headline: Baby in stroller killed in apparent stray-bullet shooting in Brooklyn
Crime & Justice.detail: A child was being pushed in a stroller at Moore and Humboldt streets in East Williamsburg on Wednesday when she suffered a gunshot wound to the head, police said.

Crime & Justice.headline: Violent rapist who preyed on women has sentence increased
Crime & Justice.detail: Bruno Sala, of Havant, Hampshire, had his sentence increased to Life Imprisonment with a minimum term of 21 years and 258 days after the Solicitor General referred his case to the Court of Appeal under the Unduly Lenient Sentence scheme, for raping and violently assaulting three women he met through dating apps.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police find drug-smuggling tunnel with rail system and cranes in Spain
Crime & Justice.detail: Police said two people headed the network, including one person considered to be the "narco-architect" and "mastermind of the tunnels."

Crime & Justice.headline: Ted Bundy's DNA linked to death of Utah teen in 1974, sheriff's office says
Crime & Justice.detail: Bundy's full DNA profiled was entered into the FBI's national database, giving investigators a shot at solving potential cases linked to Bundy.

Education.headline: Brief fire causes evacuation but no injuries at historic Detroit college building
Education.detail: A fire caused the evacuation of a historic building owned by Wayne State University in Detroit, but there were no injuries.

Education.headline: Judge says Penn must turn over information about Jewish employees in US discrimination probe
Education.detail: A judge has ordered the University of Pennsylvania to provide a list of Jewish employees to a federal agency as part of a discrimination probe.

Education.headline: Texas teen who shot teacher before killing himself seemed to be struggling in school, officials say
Education.detail: Officials report that a Texas teenager who shot a teacher before taking his own life appeared to be struggling in school.

Education.headline: No spellcheck, no delete key: Cornell students tackle writing on manual typewriters
Education.detail: Cornell University students are challenging themselves by writing on manual typewriters, foregoing the conveniences of spellcheck and delete keys.

Education.headline: Not Every Assistant Principal Wants the Top Job: 5 Views From the Field
Education.detail: An article in Education Week discusses the reasons why some assistant principals do not aspire to become principals, offering insights from the field.

Lifestyle.headline: 'I thought the war was over:' 100-year-old Iwo Jima veteran honored decades later at Disneyland
Lifestyle.detail: A 100-year-old Iwo Jima veteran was honored at Disneyland in an emotional ceremony, decades after the war.

Lifestyle.headline: Alaska Airlines unveils lie-flat suites, upgraded perks in new international business class
Lifestyle.detail: Alaska Airlines is introducing lie-flat suites and enhanced benefits in its new international business class offering.

Lifestyle.headline: Doctor reveals secrets to lasting weight loss without counting calories
Lifestyle.detail: A doctor shares insights on how to achieve sustainable weight loss without the need to count calories.

Lifestyle.headline: Cruise passengers complained about cabin problem, now ships are making a big change
Lifestyle.detail: Following passenger complaints about a cabin issue, cruise lines are implementing a significant change to address the problem.

Lifestyle.headline: The lifestyle change that can improve ill health in old age
Lifestyle.detail: Research suggests a specific lifestyle change can lead to improvements in the health of older adults.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Ukraine's perilous spring: Europe's steadfast support is more vital than ever.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial emphasizing the importance of continued European support for Ukraine as it faces a challenging spring.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the BBC's future: who decides what news means?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discussing the future of the BBC and the question of who determines the meaning of news.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Israel's death penalty: capital punishment is always wrong. This new law is doubly so.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The article discusses Israel's new law regarding the death penalty, reiterating the stance that capital punishment is inherently wrong.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: HEADLINES: Trust in govt drops as pessimism grows
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An article discussing a recent survey that shows public trust and approval ratings for the country's top leaders have declined, reflecting growing dissatisfaction and pessimism amid economic uncertainty and governance concerns.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: French president lauds 'predictability' of Europe in Japan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: French President Emmanuel Macron praised Europe's “predictability” during a visit to Japan, contrasting it with countries that “could hurt you without even informing you,” which was an apparent reference to US President Donald Trump and the US-Israeli war against Iran.

Zimbabwe.headline: Opposition to boycott CAB3 hearings after violence, intimidation
Zimbabwe.detail: The opposition party has announced they will boycott the Constitutional Amendment No. 3 (CAB3) hearings following reports of violence and intimidation during the outreach process.

Zimbabwe.headline: Surging imports erode Zim's export gains
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is experiencing a decline in export gains due to a surge in imports, impacting the country's trade balance.

Zimbabwe.headline: Cabinet approves removal of low‑value transaction fees
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean Cabinet has approved the removal of fees associated with low-value transactions.

Zimbabwe.headline: Matinyarare fires back at Mliswa amid Constitutional Amendment row
Zimbabwe.detail: Amidst the controversy surrounding the Constitutional Amendment, Matinyarare has responded to Mliswa.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe constitutional hearings rocked by abduction claims and violent clashes
Zimbabwe.detail: Public consultations on the proposed Constitutional Amendment No. 3 (CAB3) Bill have been overshadowed by reports of violence, intimidation, and alleged abductions, raising concerns about the integrity of the outreach process.
